title: Preserve three-book causal architecture
source_observation: The prologue says the epic belongs equally to the three crowned kings and inventories the full movement through Vanji
risk: Ending at Madurai would turn the Chera movement into optional aftermath
decision: Retain Pukar, Madurai, hill-country reception and Chera memorialisation in the final adaptation form
rationale: The epic's political and memorial argument depends on what happens after Madurai

title: Adult casting for principal young roles
source_observation: The poem states literary ages for Kannaki and Kovalan while the narrative contains marriage, coercion and extreme violence
risk: Literal age casting could place minors in unsafe or exploitative material
decision: Cast adult performers and treat the stated ages as literary evidence requiring historical interpretation
rationale: This preserves the text while applying modern safeguarding standards

title: Anklet continuity system
source_observation: The anklet is household property, sale object, false evidence, true evidence and the title-bearing cause
risk: A generic prop would weaken the causal chain and courtroom proof
decision: Design and track the paired anklets through material, sound, custody, contents, wrapping, sale and breaking
rationale: The object must remain legible without revealing its evidentiary difference too early
required_review: Tamil commentary; jewellery history; sound; props

title: Procedural collapse in Kovalan's execution
source_observation: The goldsmith redirects suspicion and the Pandya orders killing without investigation
risk: A single villain explanation would excuse the wider system
decision: Show the movement from market accusation through access to authority, order and execution; mark invented procedural links as interpretation
rationale: The tragedy depends on both false testimony and failed government

title: No romantic absolution of Kovalan
source_observation: Kovalan's choices contribute to wealth loss and household rupture, but his execution is unjust
risk: The film may either excuse all earlier conduct or imply that execution is punishment for private failure
decision: Keep him accountable in Pukar while presenting the Madurai killing as wrongful and disproportionate
rationale: Moral complexity and legal innocence are not opposites

title: Madhavi as artist and worker
source_observation: The epic identifies Madhavi through theatre training and devotes major cantos to performance
risk: Later moralising can reduce her to a temptress who causes the hero's fall
decision: Build her through training, artistic knowledge, labour, patronage, household pressure and emotional agency
rationale: The Pukar movement loses its social and artistic meaning if her profession is treated as contamination
required_review: Tamil performance studies; dance; music; gender history

title: Kannaki's courtroom proof precedes spectacle
source_observation: The Madurai sequence moves through suffering, city-circling and lawsuit before burning
risk: Immediate magical destruction would erase evidence and public accusation
decision: Give dramatic priority to her movement, speech, anklet handling and proof before fire
rationale: Her authority comes from truth made public, not from visual power alone

title: Burning as inhabited civic catastrophe
source_observation: The poem connects failed government to destruction of Madurai
risk: Spectacle can make mass suffering exhilarating or abstract
decision: Show differentiated inhabitants, rescue, flight, animals, water and institutional collapse; avoid triumphal destruction montage
rationale: The city must remain a human world even under divine or moral judgement
required_review: Ethics; fire safety; urban history; VFX

title: Karma does not erase agency
source_observation: The poem includes ripening past action and a former-birth curse alongside explicit government failure
risk: Karmic explanation may become victim-blaming or institutional excuse
decision: Allow characters to articulate causation without treating it as a legal verdict or moral exoneration
rationale: The adaptation must hold metaphysical and human causation together

title: Dynamic landscape treatment
source_observation: The Madurai road describes landscapes changing under season and contains agricultural and sacred knowledge
risk: Production may turn thinai into static colour-coded scenery
decision: Represent season, water, labour, route knowledge and bodily difficulty rather than fixed postcard zones
rationale: The road is a transformation of protection, ecology and status

title: Hill community as interpreting agents
source_observation: The hill people question Kannaki, witness signs, proclaim her deity and establish worship
risk: They could be reduced to an exotic chorus validating the heroine
decision: Stage their questions, deliberation, instruments, boundary-making and collective action as the mechanism of deification
rationale: Kannaki becomes socially sacred through reception, not merely through VFX
required_review: Tamil anthropology; community history; music; religion

title: No automatic medieval Chola iconography
source_observation: The epic's literary world cannot be dated or visualised simply through later Chola monuments
risk: Audience familiarity may produce gopurams, bronzes and court styles from much later periods
decision: Use an early-historic working palette and label uncertain reconstruction until specialists approve it
rationale: Chronological discipline is more important than instant recognisability

title: Performance sequences must change the story
source_observation: The epic is a continuous poem interweaving prose, song, dance and named performance forms
risk: Commercial-film treatment may detach them as interchangeable songs
decision: For every performance document performer, audience, patronage, labour, encoded knowledge and dramatic consequence before screenplay approval
rationale: Form is part of narrative causation and social world-building

title: Supernatural events remain consequential
source_observation: Kannaki's fire, divine explanation and celestial reunion belong to the epic's world
risk: Either sceptical demystification or weightless fantasy could erase sacred and ethical force
decision: Give each supernatural event a distinct cinematic grammar and foreground what it changes in choice, memory and society
rationale: Sacred meaning and cinematic restraint can coexist

title: Existing translations are canonical project layer
source_observation: Close English translations of all three books are already versioned in this repository
risk: Adaptation notes could detach from their source or silently replace it
decision: Cite book and canto in every evidence record and preserve translation files unchanged except through separate reviewed corrections
rationale: Versioned source traceability is the foundation of the project
